claimText: Under the National Mission on Natural Farming (NMNF), Bio-Input Resource Centres (BRCs) receive Rs 1 lakh in financial assistance to set up cluster-level enterprises for supplying biological inputs.
evidenceExcerpt: BRCs provide Rs 1 lakh financial assistance for setting up cluster-level enterprises to supply biological inputs.
examUse: Statement-based question on the financial assistance and purpose of Bio-Input Resource Centres.

claimText: The National Mission on Natural Farming (NMNF) is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme with a funding pattern of 90:10 for Hilly and North Eastern States and 60:40 for other States.
evidenceExcerpt: NMNF is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme with a 90:10 funding ratio for Hilly & North Eastern States and 60:40 for all other States.
examUse: Statement-based question on the scheme type and funding pattern of NMNF.
distractorHooks: Central Sector Scheme (100% funded by the Centre)
50:50 funding ratio for all other States
100% funding for Hilly and North Eastern States

claimText: The National Mission on Natural Farming (NMNF) targets reaching 1 crore farmers and initiating natural farming across a 7.5 lakh hectare area by 2025-26.
evidenceExcerpt: NMNF aims to reach 1 crore farmers and initiate Natural Farming in a 7.5 lakh Ha area by 2025-26.
examUse: Statement-based question on the specific targets and timeline of the NMNF.

claimText: Under the National Mission on Natural Farming (NMNF), 30,000 Krishi Sakhis (Community Resource Persons) are utilized to scale up natural farming practices.
evidenceExcerpt: 30,000 Krishi Sakhis (Community Resource Persons) are utilized for the easy scaling up of Natural Farming practices.
examUse: Statement-based question on the role of Krishi Sakhis in agricultural schemes.
distractorHooks: Krishi Sakhis are utilized exclusively for digital literacy among farmers
1 lakh Krishi Sakhis are deployed for chemical farming awareness
